purpose.summary: Ryewired is a virtual solderless breadboard for building, experimenting with, and learning analog audio circuits, particularly guitar effects. The goal is to replicate the workflow of prototyping a real pedal on a breadboard rather than drawing schematics.

design_principles: The application should behave like a real electronics workbench.
Behavior should favor realism and usability over exhaustive SPICE accuracy.

current_direction.next_major_milestone: Convert the board from a collection of draggable utility components into a complete pedal prototyping workstation.

workbench_model.description: The board should contain permanent infrastructure rather than requiring utility components to be placed manually.

permanent_devices.description: Permanent adjustable DC supply attached to the power rails.
Permanent LED driven by the bypass switch.

default: Input can be connected directly to Output.
Running the simulation should immediately produce audible output.
Oscilloscope and FFT should display the signal.
bypass_mode: Switch OFF routes Input directly to Output.
effect_mode: Switch ON routes Input through the user-built circuit.

workflow: Power, Input, Output, LED, and Bypass switch already exist.
Initially jumper Input directly to Output.
Construct an effect between Input and Output.
Toggle the bypass switch to compare clean and processed audio instantly.

assistant_guidelines: Read the source before proposing architectural changes.
When implementing roadmap items, preserve the realism of a physical breadboard.
